opening_thought: White noise behind my eyes. I know this room — no, I know I knew it. The walls are the right wrong distance apart.

thoughts.wake_beside_bed: Standing. When did standing happen. The bed still holds heat like something that just stopped speaking.
thoughts.locked_hint: Not yet. Some other part of me remembers before I do.
thoughts.door_locked: Layered shut. I stacked the locks myself — I just can't recall why.
thoughts.drawer_unlocked: Wood exhales. I was waiting for that sound. I think I was.
thoughts.safe_found: The wall has a second face. I hid something where I'd have to look twice.
thoughts.key_ready: Metal finds wood. The fit is older than this forgetting.
thoughts.wardrobe_open: Cold breath. Paper smell. Ink. A name I signed somewhere between sleep and waking.
thoughts.door_opening: Something unlatches that was never barred from the outside.
thoughts.desk_drawer_loot: Faces turned away. Dates that don't belong to me — except my hand wrote the receipt.
thoughts.safe_opened: Brass tongue. A blade without a handle. Rest — the word pulsing like a second heartbeat.
thoughts.bed_musings: Indentation in the linen. Someone lay here. Was it me, before I woke into not-knowing?
thoughts.clock_musings: Twelve frozen on the face. A minute buried somewhere. I circled it once — the ink remembers.
thoughts.desk_musings: Brown ring in the grain. Thoughts left half-finished. The chair still angled toward forgetting.
thoughts.sketchbook_musings: Black feathers. Lines I don't recall drawing. STILL — then an arrow I can't follow yet.
thoughts.wall_notes_musings: Paper constellation. My script on every scrap. I don't remember writing — only that it's true.
thoughts.painting_musings: Oak in oil. Roots in paint. Wind I felt somewhere that wasn't this room.
thoughts.window_musings: Blue too clean. Sun lies flat. Real places are dimmer than this.
thoughts.nightstand_musings: Grip worn smooth. Snapped off something that wanted to stay shut.
thoughts.bookshelf_musings: Dream Cartography. I was mapping exits on paper before I built one in wood.
thoughts.wardrobe_musings: Dark inside. Things I folded away when waking hurt too much to carry.
thoughts.lamp_musings: Gold pool on the desk. For one blink the room fits around me like skin.
thoughts.chair_musings: Sit. Wait. Think. The wood knows the shape my body makes when I'm almost remembering.
thoughts.door_musings: Chain. Code. Bolt. I layered every lock so only someone paying attention could leave.

calendar_clue.title: Calendar Scrap
calendar_clue.body: March 17 circled in my own handwriting. Margin note: 3:17. Why would I mark a minute?
calendar_clue.thought: March holds a minute. Time wears the first mask.

clock_solved.title: Clock Set to 3:17
clock_solved.body: The bedroom clock ticks again when set to 3:17. The desk drawer pops open.
clock_solved.thought: Three and seventeen. The hands tremble like they're ashamed to move.

photo_cipher_solved.title: Photo Backs — STILL
photo_cipher_solved.body: Rearranging the photo backs spells STILL. Receipt says Stillpoint Sleep Lab intake.
photo_cipher_solved.thought: STILL. The letters arrange themselves. My mouth wants to say what comes after.

safe_opened.title: Wall Safe
safe_opened.body: Inside the safe: a brass key blade and a note — 'Handle where you rest.'
safe_opened.thought: Rest. Where the head falls. Where the missing piece waits.

key_assembled.title: Key Assembled
key_assembled.body: Blade and wooden handle fit together. Wardrobe lock.
key_assembled.thought: Whole again. The cold wood already knows what I'm bringing.

wardrobe_open.title: Wardrobe Contents
wardrobe_open.body: Cipher disk and a folded consent letter for Project Stillpoint. My signature.
wardrobe_open.thought: Signature dried. I agreed to forget — on purpose, with open eyes.

disk_hint.title: Cipher Disk
disk_hint.body: Align STILL with POINT on the disk. The door padlock wants the full passphrase.
disk_hint.thought: STILL meets POINT. A name for the place between sleeping and waking.

door_code.title: Door Unlocked
door_code.body: Padlock code accepted. Chain slack. Deadbolt finally catches.
door_code.thought: The last layer yields. The hallway is real — or real enough to leave through.

ending_journal.title: Project Stillpoint
ending_journal.body: I volunteered for a lucid-dream containment trial. The bedroom is a reconstructed memory space — not quite real. I hid the exit passphrase from myself until I was ready to remember why I came here. The door opens. The trial ends when I choose to leave.

bed.title: Bed
bed.body: Sheets still warm. A calendar scrap peeks from under the mattress edge.

calendar_scrap.title: Calendar Scrap
calendar_scrap.body: March 17 circled. 3:17 written in the margin in my handwriting.

wall_clock.title: Wall Clock
wall_clock.body: Stopped at midnight. The second hand trembles when you touch it, like it wants a time.

desk.title: Desk
desk.body: Sketchbook, lamp, mug of pens, locked drawer. Coffee ring dried into the wood.

desk_drawer.title: Desk Drawer
desk_drawer.body: Photo backs and a Stillpoint receipt stub. The word STILL is scrambled across them.

sketchbook.title: Sketchbook
sketchbook.body: A crow study on the last page — detailed, recent. I don't remember drawing it. Margin reads: STILL → POINT.

bookshelf.title: Bookshelf
bookshelf.body: Paperbacks and a spine labeled 'Dream Cartography.' Flavor of someone who reads to escape.

nightstand.title: Nightstand
nightstand.body: A wooden key handle in the drawer. It snapped off something heavier.

key_handle.title: Key Handle
key_handle.body: Worn grip. It belongs with the blade from the safe.

painting.title: Framed Painting
painting.body: An old oil painting of a massive oak tree — branches spread wide against a pale sky. The place doesn't match anywhere you've been, but the tree feels familiar.

wall_notes.title: Pinned Notes
wall_notes.body: Cryptic sketches and half-sentences pinned across the wall — portals, spirals, diagrams. Someone mapped a maze in pencil and never took it down.

wall_safe.title: Wall Safe
wall_safe.body: Small brass safe. Inside: a key blade and a scrap — 'Handle where you rest.'

wardrobe.title: Wardrobe
wardrobe.body: Locked. The assembled key should fit the old brass latch.

cipher_disk_pickup.title: Cipher Disk
cipher_disk_pickup.body: Cardboard wheel. Windows align STILL with POINT.

stillpoint_letter.title: Stillpoint Letter
stillpoint_letter.body: Consent for Project Stillpoint — voluntary memory containment during lucid REM. My signature at the bottom.

door.title: Bedroom Door
door.body: Deadbolt spins freely until now. Chain and letter padlock block the way out.

window.title: Window
window.body: It's a paradise out there. Too bad I'm couped up in here.

combine_station.title: Desk Surface
combine_station.body: Enough space to fit two small objects together.

lamp.title: Lamp
lamp.body: A heavy metal desk lamp. It casts a warm pool of light over the desk. The bulb is hot to the touch.

chair.title: Chair
chair.body: A simple wooden desk chair. It looks comfortable enough.

ending.title: You Remember
ending.body:
You step into the hallway. Fluorescent hum. A voice on a speaker: 'Session complete.'

Project Stillpoint was never a prison — it was a chamber you built to recover a memory too sharp to carry awake. The bedroom dissolves behind you like a page turning.

You chose to forget. You chose to return.
